label: Share of PV fire events that are equipment/brush-ignition origin
source_denominator_or_scope: fire events in kWh Analytics' >$150B renewable energy loss database
useful_for: scoping the true exogenous-wildfire share as a minority residual
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. This is an ENDOGENOUS-dominant framing; the true exogenous-wildfire residual is implied to be small (~16% of fire events at most).

label: Share of PV fire loss events occurring in high-wildfire-risk areas
source_denominator_or_scope: PV fire loss events in kWh Analytics' database
useful_for: closest open proxy for a true exogenous-wildfire-area share, still not an isolated exogenous severity figure
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. Geographic risk-area proxy, not a confirmed exogenous ignition-source determination; still does not isolate DIRECT $/MW severity.

label: Share of all PV fires caused by inverters
reported_unit: % of all PV fires caused by inverters
source_denominator_or_scope: all PV fires in kWh Analytics dataset
useful_for: equipment-origin ignition-source context; explicitly out of scope for exogenous wildfire cell
caveats: Third-party paraphrase, not directly quoted from primary PDF. Confirms most PV fire is endogenous/equipment-caused, reinforcing that exogenous wildfire is a minority pathway.

label: Per-event fire severity range as % of total asset value
reported_unit: % of total asset value, per fire event
source_denominator_or_scope: not stated (severity range across sampled events, cause not isolated)
useful_for: illustrating severity dispersion only; not usable as an exogenous-wildfire-specific severity anchor
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. No sample size or cause breakdown given; blends equipment-origin and any exogenous-wildfire cases indiscriminately.

label: Average annual revenue loss from major smoke events
source_denominator_or_scope: subset of sites with generation loss among 40 locations analyzed, 1.2 GWh capacity, FLARE model
useful_for: SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark; out of scope for the physical-damage cell but corroborating for the optical/BI-adjacent layer
caveats: Smoke-soiling revenue loss, strictly separate metric family from any physical-damage figure; not a $/MW physical severity number.

label: Share of national portfolios with double-digit generation loss in 2-week smoke windows
reported_unit: % of national portfolios experiencing double-digit generation loss
source_denominator_or_scope: developers' national portfolios, 2-week smoke-event windows
useful_for: SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION frequency context
caveats: Frequency/severity distribution statistic, not a $/MW figure; separate metric family from physical damage.

label: Fire category share of total losses (count or $ basis, OCR-ambiguous)
reported_unit: % of total losses, Fire category (ambiguous axis)
source_denominator_or_scope: $1.5B total ground-up losses across 13,000+ asset-years, $100B TIV, 92GW DC, 2012-2025
useful_for: sanity-checking fire's overall share of PV losses relative to hail; denominator context
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. OCR ambiguity in source slide - two candidate percentage sets exist and are not resolved; Fire category not split wildfire vs equipment.

label: Total ground-up losses, all perils, denominator context
source_denominator_or_scope: 13,000+ asset-years / $100B TIV-years / 92GW DC / all 50 states / 2012-2025
useful_for: denominator context for the whole loss database, not fire-specific
caveats: Whole-database denominator, not isolatable to fire or wildfire specifically.

label: Weather-related losses share of all solar claims in North America (2016 report)
source_denominator_or_scope: all solar insurance claims in North America, GCube book
useful_for: historical timeline/precedent context only; wildfire named only qualitatively
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. Wildfire is only a qualitative example within the weather-related-losses bucket; not isolated as a %.

label: Wildfire share of solar extreme-weather claims
reported_unit: % of solar extreme-weather claims caused by wildfire
source_denominator_or_scope: all claims for solar asset damage due to extreme weather, GCube decade dataset (~2010-2020)
useful_for: insurer claim-share framing; endogenous/exogenous fire split not disclosed by GCube
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. GCube's claims coding does not disclose whether 'wildfire' claims are exogenous landscape fire reaching the site vs. equipment-caused fire that spread into vegetation; treat as ALL_CAUSE_PV_FIRE_BLENDED pending clarification. Do not average against kWh's ~4-8% or AXIS's ~23% - different denominators.

label: Cumulative wildfire losses to the solar industry over the decade
source_denominator_or_scope: wildfire losses to the solar industry over the decade, GCube book
useful_for: order-of-magnitude ceiling only; no per-event or per-MW figure
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. No absolute number given; unquantified range; no per-event/$/MW breakdown; endogenous/exogenous split not disclosed.

label: Texas 2022 NatCat solar losses, hail-dominated, single season
reported_unit: $ solar losses, single summer, Texas hail-driven NatCat
source_denominator_or_scope: US renewables market, single season 2022
useful_for: context only - hail-dominated bucket, wildfire not broken out
caveats: Not wildfire-specific; hail-dominated bucket used as comparator context only.

label: US renewables NatCat events attributed jointly to hail and wildfire
reported_unit: $ insured damages, individual Nat Cat events, US
source_denominator_or_scope: US renewables Nat Cat events attributed jointly to hail and wildfire, GCube book, as of April 2025
useful_for: order-of-magnitude ceiling; wildfire not isolated from hail
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. Wildfire not isolated from hail in this figure; cannot be treated as an exogenous-wildfire-specific benchmark.

label: Hail share of NatCat/extreme-weather solar gross claims, North America
reported_unit: % of total gross claim $, hail, NatCat/extreme-weather solar claims
source_denominator_or_scope: US & Canada solar NatCat and extreme-weather claims, AXIS book
useful_for: peril-mix comparator; NOT wildfire (hail-caused)
caveats: This is a HAIL figure, included only as comparator context; must never be relabeled as wildfire.

label: Hail share of gross claim $, global solar NatCat/extreme-weather claims
reported_unit: % of gross claim $, hail, NatCat/extreme-weather solar claims
source_denominator_or_scope: global solar NatCat and extreme-weather claims, AXIS book, closed claims 2019-2025
useful_for: peril-mix comparator; NOT wildfire (hail-caused)
caveats: This is a HAIL figure, included only as comparator context; must never be relabeled as wildfire.

label: $/MW gross claim, tracker stow failure (HAIL-caused, not wildfire)
source_denominator_or_scope: AXIS hail claims by stow scenario
useful_for: methodology reference for how $/MW severity CAN be reported when a peril is properly isolated - a template AXIS has not yet produced for wildfire
caveats: HAIL-CAUSED, NOT WILDFIRE. Included solely as a $/MW methodology reference showing what an isolated, well-scoped peril benchmark looks like; must never be mislabeled as wildfire severity.

label: Average ground-up downtime, NatCat/extreme-weather solar claims (includes wildfire, undifferentiated)
reported_unit: average ground-up downtime, NatCat/extreme weather solar claims (includes wildfire)
source_denominator_or_scope: AXIS solar PV claims book
useful_for: BI/downtime context; wildfire not isolated from other NatCat perils in this figure
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. Downtime figure spans all NatCat perils including wildfire, not isolated to wildfire alone; cause mix undisclosed.

label: Wildfire share of solar NatCat/extreme-weather claim losses (third-party citation)
reported_unit: % of solar NatCat/extreme-weather claim losses, wildfire
source_denominator_or_scope: AXIS renewable energy claims book (region/scope not independently confirmed)
useful_for: peril-share sanity-check only; unconfirmed vs AXIS primary text; endogenous/exogenous split not disclosed
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. Unconfirmed against primary AXIS source text retrieved separately (which gives hail 55%/wildfire 'shortly behind' with no isolated %). AXIS's claims coding does not disclose whether 'wildfire' includes equipment-caused fires that spread into vegetation.

label: Tornado share of solar NatCat/extreme-weather claim losses
reported_unit: % of solar NatCat/extreme-weather claim losses, tornado
source_denominator_or_scope: same AXIS book as wildfire 23% figure
useful_for: peril-mix comparator only, not wildfire
caveats: Not a wildfire figure; included only to show relative peril-mix context alongside the wildfire=23% citation.

label: NatCat share of global solar PV claim value (storm+hail named, wildfire not isolated)
reported_unit: % of claim value, all NatCat perils combined (storm + hail explicitly named)
source_denominator_or_scope: global solar PV insurance claims, Marsh brokerage book
useful_for: aggregate NatCat ceiling context only; wildfire's residual share within the 80% is unknown
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. Wildfire not isolated - storm and hail are explicitly named as the dominant drivers within this 80% bucket; wildfire's share is unknown and should not be assumed.

label: Average annual revenue loss from smoke events (FLARE model study)
source_denominator_or_scope: subset of sites with generation loss among 40 locations analyzed (Western/Southeast/Northeast US), 1.2 GW(?) capacity, FLARE model
useful_for: primary SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark candidate
caveats: Smoke-soiling revenue-loss figure, strictly separate metric family from physical damage; doubles the 2022 SRA's ~3% baseline estimate.

label: Share of developer national portfolios with double-digit generation loss in 2-week smoke windows
reported_unit: % of a developer's national portfolio experiencing double-digit (>10%) generation loss
source_denominator_or_scope: developers' national portfolios, during specific 2-week smoke-event windows
useful_for: SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION frequency/severity context
caveats: Frequency/severity distribution statistic, not a $/MW figure; strictly separate metric family from physical damage.

label: Derived implied ceiling for combined storms+wildfire+flooding bucket (half of $300M hail figure)
reported_unit: $ combined losses, severe storms + wildfires + flooding
source_denominator_or_scope: notable US wind and solar facilities, since 2020, GCube data
useful_for: illustrative derived sensitivity only, NOT a directly-stated wildfire-only number
caveats: all-cause PV fire, blends endogenous+exogenous ignition; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark - cannot validate a landscape-wildfire model. DERIVED, not directly stated in the source; combines wildfire with storms and flooding into a single undifferentiated bucket - cannot be treated as a wildfire-only benchmark.

label: SCE utility T&D wildfire mitigation avoided-loss case study (1-year)
reported_unit: $ avoided loss (utility T&D infrastructure, NOT solar generation)
source_denominator_or_scope: Southern California Edison operating territory, all property/utility infrastructure exposure
useful_for: cat-model mitigation-value methodology reference only; not solar-specific
caveats: Utility T&D infrastructure exposure, NOT solar PV generation assets; methodology reference only.

label: LA Firestorm Jan 2025 insured loss estimate (RMS)
reported_unit: USD insured loss (private market + CA FAIR Plan)
source_denominator_or_scope: all private-market + FAIR Plan property, fire+smoke+evacuation impacts, five fires incl. Palisades and Eaton
useful_for: industry-loss ceiling context for scaling; all-property, not solar-segmented
caveats: All-perils-combined (fire+smoke+evacuation blended), not solar-segmented; gross vs net unknown.

label: Largest insured wildfire loss, 2017 (context figure)
source_denominator_or_scope: single named historical event year (2017), all-property, not solar
useful_for: industry-scale ceiling context only
caveats: All-property, not solar-specific; exact event/denominator not itemized in extracted text.

label: LA Firestorm Jan 2025 insured industry loss estimate (Verisk/AIR)
reported_unit: USD insured industry loss, incl. CA FAIR Plan
source_denominator_or_scope: all-property, mostly residential
useful_for: industry-loss ceiling context; explicitly residential-dominated, not solar-segmented
caveats: Most losses are to residential risks per source; BI/smoke not separately broken out.

label: PCS Catastrophe designation loss threshold
reported_unit: USD threshold (designation methodology, not a loss estimate)
source_denominator_or_scope: PCS Catastrophe designation criteria, property+BI combined
useful_for: methodology/denominator context for how PCS designates a wildfire catastrophe
caveats: Designation threshold, not a loss estimate; property and BI combined per PCS convention.

label: Historical wildland-fire PCS Catastrophe count and cumulative loss (as of 2013)
reported_value: 23 events; >$8 billion cumulative; >$5 billion in most recent 10 years
reported_unit: USD, industrywide net insurance payment, all lines
source_denominator_or_scope: entire PCS wildland-fire-coded catastrophe history, all lines, national (as of 2013)
useful_for: all-property multi-event ceiling context; dated (2013), all-property, not solar-segmented
caveats: All-property (personal + commercial + auto + BI), national, dated as of 2013; not solar-segmented.

label: Fort McMurray Wildfire (May 2016) PCS-designated insured loss
source_denominator_or_scope: Alberta, Canada, all property + auto + BI lines, named single event
useful_for: single-event all-property ceiling context
caveats: All-property + auto + BI blended, not separable into DIRECT-only or solar-specific.

label: Palisades and Eaton Fires (Jan 2025) PCS-designated combined loss, updated April 2025
source_denominator_or_scope: California, all-property, PCS-designated separate catastrophe events
useful_for: single-event all-property ceiling context, most current LA-fires loss estimate cross-reference
caveats: All-perils-combined (fire with smoke/evacuation/BI blended per PCS convention); not solar-segmented.

label: Oct-Nov 2003 San Diego/San Bernardino County wildfires insured loss
source_denominator_or_scope: San Diego/San Bernardino counties, all-property, named single event (2003)
useful_for: historical single-event all-property ceiling context
caveats: All-property, not solar-segmented; historical (2003) reference figure.

label: Z-FIRE predictive lift vs regional models
reported_unit: lift ratio vs regional wildfire risk models (not a loss $)
source_denominator_or_scope: ZestyAI Z-FIRE product validation, residential/commercial lines, no solar case
useful_for: screening-tool capability context; not a loss-cost benchmark and not solar-validated
caveats: Vendor-published capability metric, not a loss-cost figure; no confirmed utility-scale solar application.

label: Palisades/Eaton fire-zone areas rated High/Very High risk by Z-FIRE (post-event validation)
reported_unit: % of affected areas rated High or Very High risk pre-event
source_denominator_or_scope: California, residential/all-structure, named-event validation
useful_for: risk-scoring model validation context; residential, not solar-validated
caveats: Model validation statistic for residential/all-structure exposure, not a solar-specific figure.

label: Destruction rate for homes with heavy near-structure vegetation vs light vegetation
reported_value: 78% destruction rate (heavy vegetation), 1.9x higher than light-vegetation homes
source_denominator_or_scope: 115,000+ North American properties, 1,200+ historical fires, residential structures
useful_for: transferable MECHANISM finding supporting vegetation-management value; residential analog, unvalidated for utility PV
caveats: Residential structure-vulnerability mechanism finding; transfer to utility-scale PV vegetation management is an analogy, not validated for PV hardware specifically.

label: Descartes parametric wildfire product maximum capacity per contract (solar named as covered industry)
source_denominator_or_scope: per-contract limit across covered industries including renewable energy/solar
useful_for: market-structure/capacity benchmark for how the parametric market currently prices/sizes wildfire risk for solar; not a loss-cost figure
caveats: Capacity ceiling, not a realized loss-cost or premium/rate-on-line figure; no solar-specific claims history disclosed.

label: Arbol parametric wildfire policy limit range
source_denominator_or_scope: per-policy limit range, Western US commercial and residential property
useful_for: market-structure/capacity comparator (lower bound) vs Descartes' $100M ceiling
caveats: Not confirmed solar-farm-eligible; no premium/rate-on-line or claims-paid figures published.

label: Arbol minimum proof-of-loss threshold
source_denominator_or_scope: per-policy minimum, Western US commercial/residential property
useful_for: market-structure floor reference
caveats: Minimum threshold, not a benchmark loss-cost figure.

label: California Valley Solar Ranch fire estimated loss (bird/electrical-arc ignition, endogenous)
source_denominator_or_scope: single event, 250 MW nameplate, BOS-only (poles/cabling), gross-or-net ambiguous
useful_for: lower-bound severity anchor for an equipment/avian-ignition PV fire event; NOT usable as an exogenous-wildfire benchmark
caveats: Ignition cause = bird/electrical arc; the fire STARTED AT the plant and burned outward through grassland - this is an ENDOGENOUS ignition-source event, not an exogenous landscape wildfire reaching the site. Excluded from the exogenous-wildfire benchmark set.

label: CVSR capacity knocked out by the endogenous-origin fire
source_denominator_or_scope: 250 MW nameplate, single event
useful_for: severity-of-outcome context for an equipment-origin fire, not exogenous wildfire
caveats: Same endogenous-ignition event as above; not an exogenous-wildfire severity datum.

label: Restoration-speed improvement, Kern County 20 MW plant, 2020 CA wildfire season
reported_value: 60 days faster restoration than competing bid
source_denominator_or_scope: single 20 MW plant event
useful_for: vendor-reported restoration-speed case study; illustrative only, not a loss-severity benchmark
caveats: Vendor-reported (EPC/O&M marketing case study); no independent verification. The plant was one of California's 9,900 wildfires in 2020, i.e., an external/landscape wildfire reached the site (exogenous), but the article frames avoided-cost/restoration-speed, not gross physical loss.

label: Restoration cost savings and avoided lost revenue, Kern County 20 MW plant
source_denominator_or_scope: single 20 MW / 200-acre plant, avoided-cost framing, not gross loss
useful_for: avoided-cost anchor only - NOT a gross exogenous-wildfire physical-damage severity figure
caveats: This is an AVOIDED-COST / restoration-savings framing, not a gross loss figure - cannot be read as a DIRECT severity benchmark even though the originating event (external wildfire reaching the site, firefighting helicopters flooding inverters) is exogenous in origin. Vendor self-reported success story (PORTFOLIO_BIAS).

label: Modules destroyed, Kern County 20 MW plant wildfire event
source_denominator_or_scope: single 20 MW plant event, exogenous wildfire reaching the site
useful_for: physical-damage unit count; no $ conversion provided, no per-MW normalization possible without module unit cost
caveats: Unit-count only, no accompanying $/module cost disclosed in source; secondary damage cause (firefighting water flooding inverters) is also present and not separable from the direct flame/heat damage.

label: Solar capacity tripped off near-instantaneously by grid-protection fault during Canyon Fire 2
reported_unit: MW tripped (near-instantaneous grid-protection fault, not sustained loss or physical burn damage)
source_denominator_or_scope: CAISO-wide solar fleet, single grid-disturbance event, subcycle overvoltage fault
useful_for: grid-response/BI-adjacent pathway context; NOT a physical-damage severity figure
caveats: This is a grid-protection TRIP (inverter ride-through response to an external/exogenous wildfire-adjacent transmission disturbance), not physical burnover damage to PV hardware. Uncited secondary source (Wikipedia); primary NERC/CAISO report not located. Do not conflate with physical-damage severity.

label: Wellington North Solar Farm capacity operating post-fire (external grass fire)
source_denominator_or_scope: single farm, single event, short-duration, external 92-hectare grass fire
useful_for: exogenous-wildfire case-study context; no $ loss figure disclosed
caveats: No damage to major equipment reported per source; no dollar figure disclosed. Genuinely exogenous (external grass fire), but severity was reportedly low and unquantified in $.

label: Mannum 2 Solar Farm containerized-inverter fire damage estimate (endogenous)
source_denominator_or_scope: single containerized-inverter fire event, Mannum 2 project, SA
useful_for: lower-bound severity anchor for a containerized-inverter equipment fire; NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark
caveats: Cause: inverter in a shipping container bursting into flames - an ENDOGENOUS equipment-origin fire, not a landscape wildfire reaching the site. Excluded from the exogenous-wildfire benchmark set despite being a genuine, disclosed dollar figure.

label: CAISO utility-scale solar generation drop vs July 2020 average, first 2 weeks Sept 2020
source_denominator_or_scope: CAISO system-wide utility-scale solar (90% of CA utility solar capacity), first 2 weeks Sept 2020
useful_for: primary SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark; highly corroborated across multiple independent sources
caveats: Smoke-soiling generation shortfall, strictly separate metric family from physical damage.

label: CAISO YoY solar generation decline despite capacity growth, Sept 2020
source_denominator_or_scope: CAISO system-wide, first half Sept 2020 vs first half Sept 2019, despite +659 MW/+5% capacity growth
useful_for: primary SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark
caveats: Smoke-soiling metric family, strictly separate from physical damage.

label: CAISO average generation decrease, September 2020 (AOD-validated)
source_denominator_or_scope: CAISO system, September 2020 (historical, AOD-validated)
useful_for: primary SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark, absolute-MW framing
caveats: Smoke-soiling metric family, strictly separate from physical damage; absolute MW, not %.

label: CAISO monthly PV generation reduction attributed to 2020 wildfire season
source_denominator_or_scope: CAISO system, 2020 wildfire scenario at current (2020-era) deployment
useful_for: primary SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark
caveats: Smoke-soiling metric family, strictly separate from physical damage.

label: Aerosol optical depth increase, California, Aug-Oct 2020
reported_value: 0.14-0.46 increase (background ~0.10; peak monthly mean 0.53 in Sept)
source_denominator_or_scope: California, Aug-Oct 2020
useful_for: smoke-intensity magnitude axis for SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION, corroborating CAISO/NREL generation-loss figures
caveats: AOD is the physical smoke-intensity input variable, not itself a loss figure; strictly SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION scope.

label: California statewide peak-hour production decline on heavy-smoke days, Sept 2020
reported_unit: % production decline vs. same days prior years
source_denominator_or_scope: California statewide, Sept 2020, noon-4pm window
useful_for: corroborating SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark, independent of CAISO/NREL
caveats: Smoke-soiling metric family; corroborates but does not independently replicate CAISO/NREL figures (same 2020 CA wildfire season).

label: Mean generation decrease per plant, 20 Australian solar farms, 2019-2020 bushfires
source_denominator_or_scope: 20 Australian utility-scale solar farms (NSW/VIC/ACT/SA), Dec 2019-Jan 2020
useful_for: cross-geography corroborating SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark (Australia, not US)
caveats: Smoke-soiling metric family; Australian geography, cross-checks the magnitude of US CAISO/NREL findings under a different bushfire event.

label: Generation decrease, Mugga Lane Solar Park (single named ACT project), 2019-2020 bushfires
source_denominator_or_scope: Mugga Lane Solar Park (single named ACT project), Dec 2019-Jan 2020
useful_for: site-level SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION anchor, Australia
caveats: Smoke-soiling metric family; single-site figure, upper end of the Australian bushfire-smoke range found.

label: Measured power-production drop across PVROM sites, western US wildfire smoke
source_denominator_or_scope: PVROM dataset sites across AZ/CA/ID/NV/NM/UT
useful_for: primary SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark (federal source, matches OSTI/Gilletly primary study)
caveats: Smoke-soiling metric family; federal-source restatement of the OSTI/Gilletly regression study - treat as corroborating, not independent.

label: California statewide/local PV output decline, September 2020
source_denominator_or_scope: California, September 2020, statewide and local
useful_for: SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION corroboration of CAISO/NREL findings
caveats: Smoke-soiling metric family; corroborates CAISO/NREL figures for the same 2020 CA wildfire season, not independent.

label: Critical heat flux for PV module ignition
reported_unit: kW/m2 (minimum heat flux below which no flame occurs)
source_denominator_or_scope: lab study, PV module laminate/backsheet, external radiant heat flux
useful_for: module-level ignition threshold - hazard-intensity input for exogenous flame/radiant exposure modeling
caveats: Lab-controlled radiant heat flux exposure, not a specific wildfire scenario; no cost/loss figure.

label: Ignition time vs heat flux curve for PV modules
source_denominator_or_scope: lab study, PV module laminate/backsheet
useful_for: ignition-time engineering curve for translating radiant exposure duration into ignition likelihood
caveats: Lab-controlled test; no field/wildfire-scenario validation; no cost/loss figure.

label: Total heat release, PV module combustion
source_denominator_or_scope: lab study, PV module laminate/backsheet
useful_for: combustion-severity engineering input, classified 'intermediate risk' per Petrella system
caveats: Lab-controlled test; no cost/loss figure.

label: Critical heat flux for single-glass/double-glazed PV module ignition (2025 corroboration)
source_denominator_or_scope: single-glass/double-glazed PV module lab study
useful_for: corroborates Yang 2015 CHF figure with a more recent module construction test
caveats: Abstract-level only, full text paywalled; corroborating not independent of Yang 2015.

label: Critical ignition heat flux, XLPE power cable (PV DC/combiner cable analog)
source_denominator_or_scope: XLPE cable, cone calorimeter lab test
useful_for: cabling ignition threshold - lower than module CHF (26 kW/m2), meaning site cabling may ignite at lower radiant exposure/greater standoff distance
caveats: Generic XLPE power cable, not confirmed as PV-specific DC cable spec; lab test, no field validation; no cost/loss figure.

label: Ignition time by radiant intensity, XLPE cable
source_denominator_or_scope: XLPE cable, cone calorimeter lab test
useful_for: cabling ignition-time engineering curve
caveats: Lab test; no cost/loss figure.

label: Canonical flame-length-probability bins (hazard-axis denominator)
reported_value: FLP1<2ft; FLP2 2-4ft; FLP3 4-6ft; FLP4 6-8ft; FLP5 8-12ft; FLP6 12+ft
source_denominator_or_scope: CONUS+AK+HI, 270m resolution, landscape conditions end of 2020
useful_for: canonical hazard-axis denominator/magnitude bins for exogenous wildfire exposure at any US location, including solar sites
caveats: Pure hazard-simulation output (BP + conditional FLP), no dollar-loss data; no PV-specific attribute; requires independent spatial join to USPVDB for solar-site-specific application.

label: Byram fireline intensity formula (magnitude axis)
source_denominator_or_scope: generic wildland fire-behavior model, requires site fuel-model inputs
useful_for: canonical magnitude axis (fireline intensity, kW/m) for exogenous wildfire severity, feeding flame-length and heat-flux-at-distance calculations
caveats: Formula only; no PV-site-specific run performed; requires independently supplied fuel/moisture/wind/slope inputs per site.

label: Heat flux and ignition-time threshold at 40m standoff from wildland flame front
source_denominator_or_scope: 20m x 50m crown-fire-scale flame front, SIAM model, wood-wall proxy
useful_for: standoff-distance screening reference; PV module CHF (26 kW/m2) is higher than Cohen's 20 kW/m2 wood-wall 'safe' threshold - an important non-transferable nuance
caveats: Wood-wall proxy, not PV-specific; PV modules have a higher CHF (26 kW/m2 per Yang 2015) than wood, so Cohen's <20kW/m2 'safe' distance for wood walls may not directly transfer to PV modules.

label: Minimum heat flux for piloted ignition at any duration
source_denominator_or_scope: wood-wall piloted ignition, any duration, SIAM model
useful_for: lower-bound ignition threshold reference
caveats: Wood-wall proxy, not PV-specific.

label: Structure survival rate, nonflammable vs flammable roof (Foote 1994, cited in Cohen 2000)
source_denominator_or_scope: Foote 1994 dataset, cited secondarily in Cohen 2000
useful_for: material-hardening effectiveness reference, transferable analog for PV site material selection
caveats: Residential structure survival statistic, secondary citation; not PV-specific.

label: Effective defensible-space treatment distance
source_denominator_or_scope: San Diego Co. chaparral, matched sample n=2,000 homes (1,000 destroyed + 1,000 surviving), 2001-2010
useful_for: vegetation-clearance/defensible-space screening variable for utility-scale PV site risk assessment
caveats: Residential homes, not PV structures; matched-sample design (not full-population regression), PORTFOLIO_BIAS applies.

label: Effective woody-cover clearance threshold
reported_unit: % woody-cover clearance immediately adjacent to structure
source_denominator_or_scope: same San Diego Co. matched sample
useful_for: vegetation-management mitigation-value threshold, transferable analog for PV perimeter management
caveats: Residential homes, not PV structures; matched-sample design.

label: Average and extreme forest-fire temperature
source_denominator_or_scope: generic forest-fire thermal reference
useful_for: thermal-exposure magnitude context for material-melting-point comparisons
caveats: Generic reference, not site-specific.

label: PV-relevant material melting/failure points
reported_value: Al 1,220F; Si 2,580F; Ag 1,760F; glass 3,092F; steel 2,700F (structural failure 1,000F)
source_denominator_or_scope: generic material science reference
useful_for: material-vulnerability thresholds for translating forest-fire temperature exposure into component failure likelihood
caveats: Generic material science, not a claims-based or site-specific figure.

label: UL 1703 module fire-rating classes (burn duration/flame spread thresholds)
reported_value: Class A 10min/~1400F/<=6ft; Class B 10min/<=8ft; Class C 4min/<=13ft
source_denominator_or_scope: UL 1703/NEC/IBC standard test criteria
useful_for: module fire-rating design/procurement specification reference
caveats: Standard test criteria, not a claims-based figure.

label: Anecdotal inverter replacement cost, 200-kW rooftop PV system
source_denominator_or_scope: anecdotal, not claims-based, cause not specified as exogenous wildfire
useful_for: single-anecdote component-cost reference only, explicitly flagged non-benchmark by the source page itself
caveats: Single anecdotal figure, not claims-based; not confirmed to be caused by exogenous wildfire (equipment-fire/failure cause not specified); rooftop, not utility-scale.

label: PV production reduction by PM2.5 level, regression model
reported_unit: % reduction in normalized production, PM2.5 50-200 ug/m3
source_denominator_or_scope: 68 eligible sites (52 utility-scale + 16 distributed), AZ/CA/ID/OR/UT, PVROM database Apr2018-Jul2019
useful_for: PRIMARY SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark - best available open source translating smoke intensity into % generation loss
caveats: PM2.5 proxy, not direct AOD or soiling-ratio measurement; small non-random 68-site sample; conflates atmospheric attenuation and any ash-soiling deposition into one coefficient.

label: Regression model fit statistics
source_denominator_or_scope: same 68-site regression model
useful_for: model-quality context for the primary PM2.5-based SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark
caveats: Model-fit statistic, not itself a loss figure.

label: 2020 wildfire-attributed monthly PV generation decrease (NREL, independent of Gilletly/OSTI)
source_denominator_or_scope: 2020 wildfire season, western US, monthly granularity
source_status: open (snippet-derived, primary PDF fetch unsuccessful this session)
useful_for: second independent SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION quantification, corroborating CAISO/NREL-CAISO-tech-report figures
caveats: Confidence downgraded to medium because the primary PDF could not be directly re-fetched in this session; snippet/webinar-transcript corroborated.

label: 2020 wildfire-attributed daily PV generation reduction (NREL)
source_denominator_or_scope: 2020 wildfire season, western US, daily granularity
useful_for: daily-granularity SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark
caveats: Snippet-derived confidence; primary PDF fetch unsuccessful this session.

label: Operating reserve requirement increase, 2020 conditions vs escalating-wildfire scenario
reported_unit: % increase in monthly operating reserve requirement
source_denominator_or_scope: 2020 wildfire season vs escalating-wildfire scenario, western US grid
useful_for: grid-integration-cost-adjacent context for SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION consequences
caveats: Grid-integration-cost adjacent metric, not a direct generation-loss or $/MW figure; snippet-derived confidence.

label: US large-scale solar PV facility count and total capacity (v4.0, April 2026)
source_denominator_or_scope: USPVDB v4.0, 49 states + DC, April 2026
useful_for: base exposure population for GIS overlay against wildfire-hazard layers
caveats: No dollar value/TIV per facility; not field-verified; no pre-joined wildfire-hazard attribute.

label: Total US solar PV capacity, January 2026 (utility + small-scale)
source_denominator_or_scope: EIA-860/860M Table 6.1.A, January 2026
useful_for: national denominator context for exposure scaling
caveats: Generic capacity denominator, no wildfire-hazard linkage.

label: California utility-scale solar capacity, June 2020 (context for 2020 wildfire season)
source_denominator_or_scope: California, June 2020, EIA Today in Energy article
useful_for: state-level exposure denominator for the 2020 CA wildfire smoke-soiling literature
caveats: Denominator context for the CAISO/NREL/NCAR 2020 smoke-soiling figures; not a wildfire-hazard or loss figure itself.

label: Canonical flame-length-probability bins (hazard-axis denominator, cross-referenced from R4)
reported_value: FLP1<2ft; FLP2 2-4ft; FLP3 4-6ft; FLP4 6-8ft; FLP5 8-12ft; FLP6 12+ft
source_denominator_or_scope: CONUS+AK+HI, 270m resolution, landscape conditions end of 2020
useful_for: same canonical hazard-axis denominator as USFS_FSIM_RDS_2016_0034_3 (R4); cross-referenced, not double-counted
caveats: Same underlying dataset as the R4 FSim entry; listed once here for R5's exposure-denominator context, not an independent number.

label: Utility solar project sited in CAL FIRE High/Very-High FHSZ (project-level example)
reported_value: 643-acre solar facility, majority in High FHSZ
source_denominator_or_scope: San Diego County EIR, project-level CEQA disclosure
useful_for: illustrative project-level screening evidence; not a portfolio-wide GW figure
caveats: Project-level, acreage-based (not MW), not systematically compiled across all CA utility solar; no dollar-loss data.

label: US wildfires and acres burned, 2025
source_denominator_or_scope: NIFC national annual statistics, 2025, all-property, all-cause
useful_for: generic denominator/context, no solar linkage
caveats: All-property, all-cause; no solar linkage.

label: Peak annual federal wildfire suppression cost, 2021
source_denominator_or_scope: NIFC annual federal suppression cost series, 2021 (highest year)
useful_for: government cost context, not property loss, no solar linkage
caveats: Government suppression cost, not property loss; no solar linkage.

label: Camp Fire (2018) structures destroyed, deaths, acreage - most destructive CA wildfire on record
source_denominator_or_scope: CAL FIRE Top-20 Most Destructive California Wildfires ranking
useful_for: all-property historical ceiling context, no solar linkage
caveats: All-property, not solar-specific.

label: California Firestorm 2018, CPI-adjusted cumulative loss - largest single billion-dollar wildfire event 1980-2024
source_denominator_or_scope: Western Wildfires/California Firestorm, Summer-Fall 2018, all-property
useful_for: all-property historical ceiling context, no solar linkage
caveats: All-property, all-cause; no solar breakout in the NOAA series.

label: Palisades and Eaton Fires (Jan 2025) insured loss - largest-ever insured wildfire loss event
source_denominator_or_scope: Los Angeles County, Jan 2025, explicitly residential-dominated per Swiss Re framing
useful_for: reinsurance-industry record-setting ceiling context, no solar linkage
caveats: Explicitly framed as concentrated in high-value single-family residential property; no asset-class breakout; no solar attribution.

label: Palisades and Eaton Fires (Jan 2025) total economic and insured loss (Munich Re, cross-check of Swiss Re)
source_denominator_or_scope: Los Angeles County, Jan 2025, no asset-class breakout
useful_for: cross-reinsurer corroboration of the LA wildfire record ceiling, no solar linkage
caveats: No asset-class or peril-mechanism (flame vs smoke vs BI) breakout; no solar or energy-infrastructure line item.

label: Beryl solar farm grass fire burning under panels (cause undetermined, landscape-type)
reported_value: 110 MW farm; fire burned under panels; site shut down/isolated
source_denominator_or_scope: single farm, single event, cause undetermined but landscape/grass-fire type spreading under the array
useful_for: exogenous-wildfire case-study context; no dollar figure or physical-damage-severity number disclosed
caveats: No cost, no damage-severity percentage, and no confirmed ignition source disclosed; press report only.

label: Five Victorian solar farms ordered to cease generation over bushfire/vegetation risk
reported_value: 5 solar farms shut down, effective midday 22 Feb 2025
reported_unit: count of farms; regulatory action, not a loss event
source_denominator_or_scope: regulatory shutdown order, Victoria, Australia; only one of the five (Raywood, 20 MW) has a known individual capacity
useful_for: regulatory/preventive-action context showing wildfire-vegetation risk is being actively managed by regulators; not a loss or damage figure
caveats: This is a REGULATORY PREVENTIVE SHUTDOWN, not a wildfire loss event; no aggregate MW or dollar figure for the 5-farm group disclosed; Raywood (one of the five) is separately classified ENDOGENOUS (inverter-origin fire).

label: Grass fire at a Kings County, CA solar panel farm (cause under investigation)
source_denominator_or_scope: single event, Kings County, CA, cause under investigation at time of report
useful_for: exogenous-wildfire case-study context; no dollar figure, no MW/capacity figure, no confirmed damage severity
caveats: Cause was under investigation at time of report (could be exogenous grass fire or equipment-origin spreading into grass); no cost or capacity-loss figure disclosed; local news source only.

label: Raywood solar farm inverter fire spreading into grass beneath panels (endogenous)
reported_value: 20 MW capacity; fire started in inverter, spread into grass under panels
source_denominator_or_scope: single farm, single event, Victoria, Australia; ignition confirmed to have started in an on-site inverter
useful_for: equipment-ignition-source case study; the fire then spread into vegetation on-site, illustrating how endogenous fires can create secondary vegetation-fire risk, but this is NOT an exogenous landscape wildfire reaching the site
caveats: Ignition confirmed to be an ON-SITE inverter, i.e., ENDOGENOUS; the subsequent vegetation ignition is a secondary/downstream effect of an equipment fire, not an external wildfire reaching the plant. Excluded from the exogenous-wildfire benchmark set. No dollar figure disclosed.

label: CONUS-wide baseline monthly-mean GHI decrease from transported/dilute wildfire smoke
reported_unit: % decrease in baseline monthly-mean GHI vs low-smoke-year average
source_denominator_or_scope: CONUS-wide (all lower-48 states), transported/dilute smoke conditions, peer-reviewed study, 2025
useful_for: broadest-geography SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION benchmark - national baseline dilute-smoke effect, complementing the CA/AU concentrated-smoke-event figures
caveats: This is a GHI (irradiance) measure, not a direct PV generation-loss %; dilute/background smoke effect, much smaller than the acute CA 2020 (30%) or AU 2019-2020 (4-12%) concentrated-smoke-event figures; strictly SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION scope, separate from physical damage.

label: Structure destruction counts, Witch and Guejito Fires (2007), Rancho Bernardo area (residential analog)
reported_value: 274 homes in fire path; 245 within final perimeter; 74 destroyed, 16 damaged (Rancho Bernardo); 1,125 destroyed/77 damaged (Witch Fire overall)
source_denominator_or_scope: San Diego County, CA, 2007, residential structures, NIST post-fire forensic investigation
useful_for: residential structure-vulnerability engineering analog; transferable methodology for utility PV structure-survival analysis, not a PV-specific figure
caveats: Residential structures, not PV assets; provides an engineering analog for exogenous-wildfire structure-survival analysis methodology, not a directly transferable dollar or %-damage figure for solar.

label: Hazard Mitigation Methodology vulnerability/option counts and structure-separation bands
reported_value: 40 vulnerabilities; 57 hardening options; 8 categories; Structure Separation Distance: Low Density >50ft, Moderate 25-50ft, High Density <25ft
source_denominator_or_scope: NIST Hazard Mitigation Methodology (HMM), generic structure-hardening framework
useful_for: structure-separation-distance engineering reference, transferable analog for utility PV array spacing/setback risk assessment
caveats: Generic structure-hardening framework, not PV-specific; no cost or claims data attached.

label: Fence-to-structure ignition experiments, full-scale testing
reported_value: 187 full-scale experiments; fences 91cm (3ft) apart still produced large flames
source_denominator_or_scope: NIST full-scale fence/mulch ignition-pathway testing, generic (non-PV) built environment
useful_for: combustible-adjacency engineering reference, transferable analog for PV site perimeter/fencing material choices
caveats: Generic fence/mulch ignition-pathway study, not PV-specific; no cost or claims data attached.

label: 100kW fixed-tilt PV O&M cost breakdown by subsystem (denominator/context for replacement-cost shares)
reported_value: Inverter 31%; Cleaning/Vegetation 19%; PV Array 13%; Electrical 10%; Documents 8%; PV Module 7%; DC Wiring 5%; Mechanical 4%; Asset Mgmt 2%; AC Wiring 1%; Monitoring 0%
reported_unit: % of annual O&M cost by subsystem
source_denominator_or_scope: 100kW fixed-tilt reference system, NREL O&M best-practices report
useful_for: subsystem-value-share denominator context; NOT a wildfire-damage figure, but useful for understanding which subsystems (e.g., inverter, vegetation) dominate cost and thus loss-severity potential
caveats: This is an O&M COST breakdown (routine maintenance spend), not a wildfire-damage or claims figure; included only as denominator/subsystem-weighting context. Vegetation management is 19% of O&M cost, corroborating why defensible-space/vegetation-clearance mechanisms matter for wildfire risk.

label: Recommended annual O&M cost benchmark
source_denominator_or_scope: NREL O&M best-practices report, generic PV fleet, not wildfire-specific
useful_for: generic O&M cost denominator context, not a wildfire or damage figure
caveats: Routine O&M cost benchmark, unrelated to wildfire damage or claims; included only as general-denominator context.

label: Wildfire Hazard Potential: burn probability and conditional flame length ranges (2024 release)
reported_value: Burn probability (BP) 0-0.14; conditional flame length (CFL) 0-861.7 ft
source_denominator_or_scope: CONUS, 30m resolution, USFS Wildfire Risk to Communities, landscape conditions as of end of 2020
useful_for: canonical hazard-axis denominator (companion dataset to FSim RDS-2016-0034-3), usable for GIS overlay against USPVDB solar-site locations
caveats: Pure hazard-simulation output, no dollar-loss or PV-specific attribute; requires independent spatial join to USPVDB for solar-site-specific application; also includes flame-length-exceedance-probability layers (FLEP4, FLEP8) not separately itemized here.
